cards.front: What happens to an op-amp when operated Open-Loop?
cards.back: Due to massive gain (e.g. 10^5), a tiny voltage difference between inputs causes the output to immediately saturate at the supply rails.

cards.front: What is the function of a basic comparator?
cards.back: It compares an analog input voltage against a reference voltage, snapping its output to one of two digital states based on which is higher.
A comparator with its reference voltage tied to 0V (Ground). It converts analog AC waves into digital square waves triggering exactly at 0V.

cards.front: What is the severe practical weakness of a basic comparator?
cards.back: Chattering. If a noisy analog signal dawdles near the threshold, it will cross it multiple times rapidly, causing the output state to violently flip back and forth.

cards.front: How does a Schmitt Trigger differ from a basic comparator?
cards.back: A Schmitt Trigger uses Positive Feedback (output wired to the non-inverting terminal) to create Hysteresis, solving the noise chattering problem.

A property where a system has different thresholds depending on its current state. A rising signal must cross a higher threshold (V_TU) to trigger; a falling signal must cross a lower threshold (V_TL).

cards.front: Does the Virtual Short principle (V_+ = V_-) apply to a Schmitt Trigger?
cards.back: NO. Virtual Short relies entirely on Negative Feedback. Schmitt triggers use Positive feedback, intentionally driving the inputs far apart.

cards.front: In a Schmitt Trigger with a resistor divider from output (V_o) to V_+, how is the Upper Threshold Voltage (V_TU) calculated?
cards.back: V_TU = (+V_sat) * [ R_2 / (R_1 + R_2) ], where R_2 is the resistor to ground and +V_sat is the positive supply saturation limit.
The voltage gap between the Upper Threshold (V_TU) and Lower Threshold (V_TL). Any noise smaller than this band is ignored by the circuit.

cards.front: Is a Schmitt Trigger considered an analog or digital circuit?
cards.back: Analog and Digital! It acts as an interface. It takes an analog continuous input and outputs a strict two-state digital output.
